destinations.evidence: Delivery submission rejects Fulfilled entries and only transitions AwaitingDelivery entries to DeliveryReady.
The separate canonical Phase 1 settlement path is responsible for moving Fulfilled entries to AwaitingDelivery.

equivalence_argument: The lifecycle requires an explicit Phase 1 settlement transition before Phase 2 delivery; delivery submission no longer auto-promotes Fulfilled entries and therefore cannot race canonical settlement.
rationale: The merge removes the previous recovery path that auto-transitioned Fulfilled to AwaitingDelivery on delivery submission and replaces it with a rejection explaining that Phase 1 settlement must be finalized first. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Submitting a Phase 2 delivery result should be accepted only when the async pool entry is already in AwaitingDelivery; a Fulfilled entry must not be auto-transitioned and should return InvalidState so executors cannot submit delivery before Phase 1 settlement is canonicalized.
suggested_falsification_tests: Put a two-phase async entry in Fulfilled state and call submit_delivery_result, asserting InvalidState and no delivery data is stored; call mark_awaiting_delivery first, then submit delivery and assert the entry transitions to DeliveryReady.
summary: Phase 2 delivery requires canonical AwaitingDelivery state.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Place a two-phase async entry in Fulfilled and call submit_delivery_result_with_context; assert InvalidState(Fulfilled) and no delivery data is stored, then mark_awaiting_delivery and assert delivery succeeds.
title: Delivery results require canonical Phase 1 settlement
